commit.message:
fix: downgrade permanent redirects (301→302, 308→307) to prevent browser caching

Browsers cache 301 Moved Permanently and 308 Permanent Redirect
responses indefinitely at the OS/browser level.  The Location URLs
emitted by maskproxy contain proxy-internal paths (/__sd__/…) and
localhost addresses that are session-specific.  If cached and the proxy
is restarted, switched to a different target, or the user opens a fresh
browser tab, those stale cached redirects cause redirect loops or point
to the wrong host.

Fix: in modifyResponse, downgrade 301→302 and 308→307 so the browser
always re-asks the proxy on the next visit instead of serving the
redirect from its internal cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix expander arrow CSS — cursor, pointer-events, SVG size, markers

The arrow/toggle on st.expander (Geo Refinement, IO Queue) was
unresponsive on Streamlit Cloud. Root cause: global font-family
!important on all elements + missing cursor/pointer-events rules
broke the clickable summary element.

Fixes:
- summary { cursor: pointer } — ensures clickable cursor
- summary svg { pointer-events: none; min-width/height: 16px }
- details { pointer-events: auto } — ensures container is clickable
- ::marker, ::-webkit-details-marker { display: block } — shows arrow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correctly handle ccTLD targets in computeRootDomain

When the target is a 3-label ccTLD domain like ynet.co.il, bbc.co.uk,
or domain.com.au, the previous heuristic stripped the first label and
returned co.il / co.uk / com.au as the "root domain".  This caused the
subdomain masking regex to match ALL *.co.il domains (not just
*.ynet.co.il), routing unrelated external sites (analytics, CDNs,
third-party trackers) through the /__sd__/ mechanism.  Those external
sites had their own redirect chains which, when processed through the
proxy with user string replacements, created redirect loops that
triggered ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS in Chrome.

Fix: add a knownSLDs lookup table of well-known second-level domain
labels (co, com, net, org, gov, ac, edu, …).  When stripping the leading
label from a 3-label host would produce a 2-label co.XX / com.XX style
suffix, the original hostname is already the registrable domain and is
returned unchanged.

Examples:
  ynet.co.il  → ynet.co.il  (was: co.il)
  bbc.co.uk   → bbc.co.uk   (was: co.uk)
  domain.com.au → domain.com.au (was: com.au)
  www.ynet.co.il → ynet.co.il  (unchanged — 4 labels, strips www)

Also fix the startup log to always print the subdomain masking scope,
including when rootDomain == targetHost (e.g. ynet.co.il, github.com).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 5: UX logic fixes — PMP pricing, PG CPM, defaults, scroll

- PMP: deal setup first (auction type, pricing, buyer), then CPM conditionally
  - Market Price: hides CPM input, shows 'no floor required'
  - Floor/Fixed: shows CPM input with appropriate label
- PMP: always 'Create New' deal — removed 'Existing ID' option
- PMP: queue card shows 'Market / No floor' instead of 0.00
- PG: removed misleading rate card reference from caption
  - CPM defaults to 0.01 (forces planner to enter real value)
  - Help text: 'Enter the negotiated CPM from the media plan'
- PG audit: pass/fail only (no warn against internal rate defaults)
- Default product: PMP_STANDARD (was FIRST_IMP_PRIORITY_PMP)
- Default market: US (already was, confirmed)
- Scroll reduction: IO Queue & Rules wrapped in expander
  - Auto-expands when queue has items
  - Compact queue cards (less padding/margins)

commit.message:
Add verbose logging and log-file support

New flags:
  -verbose    log every request/response with full headers + body preview
              (4 KiB cap, sensitive headers redacted: Authorization, Cookie, etc.)
  -log <path> tee all output to a file (append mode) in addition to stderr

Default mode now logs one line per request/response:
  -> GET    https://upstream/path
  <- 200    https://upstream/path  (14.2 KB, 42ms)

New file: logger.go — Logger struct wrapping log.Logger with:
  Printf / Fatal (always shown)
  Verbosef (verbose-only)
  LogRequest(req, bodySnapshot) time.Time
  LogResponse(resp, bodySnapshot, start)
  bodyDump, sortedKeys, humanBytes helpers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v0.2.66: Auto-purge stale overdue instances on new cycle

pending/overdue instances from previous cycles (due_date < today)
are deleted first. Keeps the schedule clean without accumulation.

commit.message:
fix: prevent scroll cascade clipping TabBar and AI Chat header on insert

When clicking 'insert to editor' in AI Chat, browser's scrollIntoView
cascaded through overflow:hidden containers (.workspace, .workspace-region),
shifting both TabBar and AI Chat header upward (upper half clipped).

Three-layer defense:
1. CSS: overflow:clip on .workspace-region (already on .workspace) -
   prevents browser from scrolling these containers at all
2. React onScroll handlers on both containers - resets scrollTop/Left
   to 0 as JS fallback for older WebKit without clip support
3. scrollEditorToSelection walks ancestors after adjusting .cm-scroller,
   resetting any unexpected scroll offset on overflow:hidden parents

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Simplify getCollection switch statement in PostLayout (#15)

with a single getCollection(entry.collection) call. entry.collection is

purely repetitive. Behaviour is identical; the cast to (typeof entry)[]
is preserved as before.

* Update tests/test_alias_template_global_scope_ret42.cpp

Co-authored-by: devin-ai-integration[bot] <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

Skip the :: token without appending it to the name builder.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Gregor Gullwi <gregor.gullwi@gmail.com>

commit.message:
feat(ozone): phase 1G — frontend-choice on-ramp to ozone+

Add an interactive FrontendChoice screen to the ozone launcher so users
can pick SillyTavern or ozone+ after confirming a launch plan:

- Add Screen::FrontendChoice and FrontendMode (SillyTavern | OzonePlus)
  to src/ui/mod.rs; screen sits between Confirm and Launching
- Add render_frontend_choice() to src/ui/launcher.rs — centred list
  with ↑↓ navigation, Enter to confirm, Esc to go back
- Add --frontend <MODE> CLI flag to ozone binary (sillyTavern | ozonePlus)
  to bypass the interactive screen from scripts/CI
- Backend (KoboldCpp) is always started first regardless of choice
- ozone+ path: disable_raw_mode + LeaveAlternateScreen, then execv() to
  ozone-plus list so the terminal is clean and no subprocess overhead
- SillyTavern path: unchanged (open browser, enter Monitor screen)
- Update .mex/ROUTER.md to mark Phase 1 complete
- Add .mex/patterns/ozoneplus-phase1g-launcher-onramp.md

All cargo gates green: check, 6+8 tests, clippy -D warnings (both crates).

commit.message:
Implement Orphek integration with enhanced device discovery and setup (#1)

* feat: implement Orphek integration with support for OR4-iCon LED Bar

- Add GitHub Actions workflows for pull requests and releases
- Create configuration flow for device setup
- Implement API client for device communication
- Update coordinator and light platform to manage device state
- Enhance documentation and troubleshooting guide
- Update manifest and translation files for new features

* working with tuya app

* Refactor Orphek integration to support Orphek account login and improve device discovery

- Updated config flow to allow login via Orphek account (email/password) instead of Tuya Cloud credentials.
- Implemented new schemas for user input and device discovery.
- Enhanced device discovery to include both LAN and cloud devices, handling local keys more effectively.
- Added new sensor entities for temperature, mode, and schedule information.
- Improved error handling and user feedback during setup.
- Updated documentation strings and translations to reflect changes in setup process.
- Added support for additional features in the light entity, including effects.

* refactor: streamline Orphek login flow and remove unused Tuya integration

* Potential fix for pull request finding

feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool (#4609)

* Initial plan

* feat(acceleration): CUDA HNSW visited array memory scaling with persistent pool

Implements roadmap item #219 (v1.9.0):

- Remove per-invocation cudaMalloc/cudaFree from launchHnswSearchKernel();
  caller now provides pre-allocated d_visited pool (nullptr → overflow signal)
- Add persistent visited bitset pool to CudaHnswTraversalEngine::Impl:
  allocated once in buildIndex() at maxBatchSize × ceil(numNodes/8) bytes,
  freed in freeDevice()
- Implement chunked batch processing in batchSearch(): when pool_capacity <
  num_queries, split into sub-batches of pool_capacity, process serially,
  concatenate on host. Covers both single-pass and multi-pass (large-k) paths.
- Expose CudaHnswTraversalEngine::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n) and
  CUDAVectorBackend::setMaxBatchSize(size_t n)
- Clamp pool to 90% of BackendCapabilities::maxMemoryBytes in buildHnswAnnIndex()
- Set BackendHealthStatus::makeDegraded() via setError() when pool allocation
  fails so getHealthStatus() surfaces the issue to callers
- Add 14 new tests: setMaxBatchSize, hasVisitedPool, chunked correctness,
  degraded health on pool failure
- Update FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md and ROADMAP.md to mark item #219 done

commit.message:
fix: update echo-tracking fields only after successful Ynison send

Guard _send_progress_to_ynison on _ynison.connected and move
_last_sent_to_ynison_ms/_time updates after the actual send so
silent no-ops during disconnect don't suppress later real updates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Make HTTPS the default; rename flags for clarity

- HTTPS is now the default upstream scheme (no flag needed)
- -insecure: use plain HTTP to upstream (previously required -ssl for https)
- -skip-verify: skip TLS cert verification for self-signed certs
  (previously -insecure)
- -ssl: removed
- Updated usage text, README examples, and log messages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: auto-calculate tool count + ROI pricing from TOML

P0 automation fixes:
- tool_count now auto-calculated from toolkit_nav.toml (was hardcoded 26)
- Never needs manual bumping when adding/removing tools
- ROI Calculator pricing now reads from data/roi_pricing.toml via Hugo
- JS falls back to hardcoded values if TOML injection fails
- Removed tool_count from hugo.toml (no longer needed)
